text:
This article is about the Evangelion from the Neon Genesis Evangelion TV series. For the Evangelion from Rebuild of Evangelion film series, see Evangelion Unit-00 (Rebuild).

 is the first functional Evangelion unit created, and is referred to as "EVA-00 PROTO TYPE" due to being the prototype for the rest of the Evangelion series. It is piloted by Rei Ayanami.

Profile

Evangelion Unit-00 is the first successful Evangelion, and is piloted by the First Child, Rey Ayanami. Its helmet design is similar to the previous failed Evangelion prototypes seen in the Eva Graveyard, albeit with only one "camera." The helmet also contains a green dome on the top which glows upon activation. Unit-00 is the only Evangelion of the main trio that is never seen without its armor restraints (Unit-01 being seen on multiple occasions, and Unit-02 in The End of Evangelion), and the appearance of its organic body is thus unknown.

Unit-00's armor was initially colored a yellowish-orange like the previous prototypes, but it was heavily damaged by the Angel Ramiel's particle beam during Operation Yashima. The subsequent repairs and upgrades included blue armor plating in addition to other alterations in order to make it more suitable for combat conditions. This upgrade is referred to as .

These upgrades make it greater resemble the other Evangelion units; in particular, the addition of shoulder pylons. Though in the battle against Matarael, these pylons are shown to hold retro-rockets, and in the battle against Sahaquiel, it is also shown that it has a Progressive Knife similar in appearance to Unit-01's. Either its pylons hold both equipment, or these were simply different equipment loadouts. Due to its prototype status however, it cannot be fitted with D-Type Equipment.

Unit-00 tends to be the most unstable of the Evangelions, going berserk in several tests to the detriment of the pilot. The true nature of Unit-00's soul is never specified, but it is heavily implied to be directly connected to Rei in some fashion.

Weapons

N² bomb
Pallet Rifle
Positron Sniper Rifle
Progressive Knife
Sniper Rifle
Spear of Longinus

History

Neon Genesis Evangelion

Evangelion Unit-00 was the first successful Evangelion created after a series of failed prototypes. It was first seen in an activation test being piloted by Rei Ayanami. However, it went berserk, attempting to destroy the control room before auto-ejecting its entry plug, injuring Ayanami. It eventually ran out of power and was contained with bakelite. Episode:05 

Unit-00 later underwent a second activation test, where Ayanami was able to successfully sync with it. Afterwards, it was approved for combat in Operation Yashima against the Angel Ramiel. It was sortied alongside Unit-01, where it was equipped with an improvised heat shield constructed from the underside of a space shuttle. It used this shield to successfully defend Unit-01 against Ramiel's particle beam, allowing the Angel to be sniped by a shot from the Positron Sniper Rifle. However, it ended up heavily damaged by the beam's heat during the encounter. Episode:06 

The unit was later repaired and upgraded into by the time Matarael attacked Tokyo-3. It worked in tandem alongside Unit-01 and Unit-02, taking a support role in the operation. With the aide of its retro-rockets, it dropped down a long shaft to retrieve a Pallet Rifle and tossed it up to Unit-01, which used it to fatally shoot the Angel. Episode:11 

Against Sahaquiel, Unit-00' took part in a three-way operation with the other Evangelions by running to intercept the falling Angel. Unit-00' helped neutralize Sahaquiel's A.T. Field, and then used its Progressive Knife to cut through the Field, allowing Unit-02 to stab the Angel's core and destroy it. Episode:12 

Unit-00' was then involved in a pilot interoperability test where Shinji Ikari attempted to sync with it. However, the unit went berserk in a similar manner to its first activation test, attempting to destroy the control room, although it did not auto-eject the entry plug. It then powered down, and was later assigned another activation test with Ayanami to ensure it was still capable, which was presumably a success. It was then used to take the Spear of Longinus into Terminal Dogma, where it used the Spear to restrain Lilith. Episode:14 

Unit-00' was later sortied with the other Evangelions against Leliel, but Unit-01 ended up being quickly swallowed by the Angel's Dirac Sea. Unit-00' attempted to shoot the Angel with its sniper rifle, but was unsuccessful. It later prepared to participate in a rescue operation alongside Unit-02, but Unit-01 managed to kill Leliel before the operation took place. Episode:16 

The three Evangelions were also sortied against Evangelion Unit-03, which had become taken over by the Angel Bardiel. The Angel first neutralized Unit-02, and was later left within vulnerable firing range against Unit-00'. However, Ayanami hesitated firing due to knowing that the Fourth Child was still inside Unit-03, and ended up being surprise attacked. Bardiel attempted to infiltrate Unit-00' via leaking itself into the left arm, but the arm was remotely severed under Gendo Ikari's orders, leaving both the unit and Ayanami wounded before Bardiel moved on to Unit-01. Episode:18 

After Zeruel broke into the GeoFront and incapacitated Unit-02, Ayanami sortied Unit-00' despite its left arm not being repaired. She attempted a suicide run with an N² bomb in order to destroy the Angel; managing to get up close and penetrate Zeruel's A.T. Field before exploding the N² bomb. However Zeruel ended up unaffected by the explosion, which blew off Unit-00's remaining arm, and crippled the Evangelion by slicing its head in two. Episode:19 

It was later fully repaired and partook in the battle against Arael. It attempted to use the Positron Sniper Rifle (with less power than during Operation Yashima) to snipe the Angel in satellite orbit, but the shot was blocked by the Angel's A.T. Field. The decision was then made to use the Spear of Longinus, with Unit-00' descending into Terminal Dogma to remove it from Lilith. It then threw the Spear into orbit, obliterating Arael; though this caused the Spear to become near irrecoverable as it settled into lunar orbit. Episode:22 

Unit-00's final sortie occurred during the battle with the sixteenth Angel, Armisael. Armisael suddenly attacked the lone unit and easily penetrated its A.T. Field, resisting point-blank sniper rifle shots before attempting to bio-fuse with the Evangelion. In order to prevent the same from happening to Unit-01, which was sortied to provide support, Ayanami absorbed Armisael into Unit-00's core to contain it. She then self-destructed the unit, which momentarily took the form of a large white Rei before exploding. Its entry plug was later found on the site, heavily damaged, but the pilot did not survive. Episode:23 

In Other Media

Neon Genesis Evangelion manga

In the manga, Unit-00 plays a near identical role as in the anime, including being self-destructed by Ayanami to destroy Armisael.

Since the manga is only black and white, it is unclear if it is ever repainted from yellowish-orange to blue like in the anime. The shading on the armor in the black and white panels remains unchanged from its first appearance (shown on the second collection cover to be yellow) and its lighter shading compared to the other units suggests its color to be lighter then the other units, which would not be the case if it was recolored from bright yellowish-orange to dark blue. 

With that said, in Volume 2, Sadamoto depicts Unit-00 in the recolored dark blue. While the chapter art is not related to the story, it is possible the inclusion was made to signify the change from the yellowish-orange to blue color scheme.

Neon Genesis Evangelion: ANIMA

In the Neon Genesis Evangelion: ANIMA series, after the self-destruction of Unit-00 during the battle against Armisael, its core was preserved and stored in Central Dogma. It was rebuilt using leftover parts from the original Unit-00 and F-Type parts, and repurposed as the specialized sniper unit Evangelion Unit-00 Type F.

Trivia

"Zerogōki" does not follow the naming system used for the succeeding Evas. Under that system Unit-00 would have been enunciated as "Reigōki" rather than using the English word "zero," which would have given both it and Rei practically the same name.
Officially the English name is EVA-00 PROTO TYPE, with the word "prototype" being separated.
Briefly-seen readouts of Unit-00 in the anime indicate that it actually has two eyes, both a left and right. It is unknown if this actually the intention, or if this is an error.

In the Sore o Nasumono: Neon Genesis Evangelion Concept Design Works book, the original design proposed by Ikuto Yamashita was drastically different from the design used by the TV series, having similar color separation as the design used by Evangelion Proto Type-00. It also includes the shoulder pylons and it is mostly yellow, white and gray unlike the one used in the TV series, which is mostly yellow. Sore o Nasumono: Neon Genesis Evangelion Concept Design Works p.15 
Unit 00's refit design can be seen during the studio scene as a prop costume in Evangelion: 3.0+1.0 Thrice Upon A Time.
